insight: Ebb Turbine is considered an underrated support unit that is almost always worth buying one of when in blue, but players rarely want more than one. The unit's low impact stems from its nature as a support unit rather than being weak in absolute terms.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about whether Ebb Turbine needs a buff and what form that buff should take.

insight: Lancetooth (referred to as 'tooth' in discussion) is considered efficient enough that games can often be decided by just the first two teeth. Players typically stop buying at around three teeth, and the first tooth's efficiency at single blue cost with double absorb denial is seen as potentially overtuned.
author: consensus
context: Discussion on whether Lancetooth needs a supply nerf, gold nerf, or cost change, with masn6811 and silentslayers agreeing that most games only require the first two teeth.

insight: Scorchilla is considered a set-dominating unit because of its efficiency as an initial attacker, its ability to deny absorb, and the fact that it can also absorb 2 damage itself. The proposed nerf reduces its HP from 3 to 2 to make it less all-around powerful without changing its offensive role.
author: silentslayers
context: silentslayers posted patch notes proposing Scorchilla HP nerf from 3 to 2, with community noting no strong objections.

insight: Ferritin Sac is not viable in the BSO (base set only) context because even when the opponent plays TTD and you use Ferritin, you cannot win even if the opponent also never buys Ferritin. This was confirmed through extensive testing by masn6811, and silentslayers reports matching masn's viability test results at a 99% rate.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion on whether Ferritin Sac needs a buff, with masn6811 explaining that testing showed it fails the standard viability test (p2 BSO win condition).

insight: Ferritin Sac's build time of 2 creates a specific awkwardness: if the opponent opens with two Tarsiers, buying Ferritin Sac on turn 1 means you cannot get a Wall in time to defend, so you are effectively forced to buy a Blastforge anyway. This means Ferritin Sac does not replace Blastforge in reactive defensive lines.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of Ferritin Sac build-time issues and whether a BT1 version would be too powerful.

insight: Chrono Filter changing its resource production to a click ability (exhaust on click, giving BR) is considered a strict but appropriate buff by both elyot and masn6811. At top-level play it is barely a buff since players click it anyway, but it makes the unit significantly more forgiving for less experienced players and in edge cases like Arka standoffs.
author: consensus
context: Terra Reveene proposed changing Chrono Filter to give BR on click rather than passively, and elyot, masn6811, and silentslayers all approved.

insight: Elyot (developer) disfavors strong soak units because they drag out game length, citing Chief and Doomed Mech as examples that were nerfed for that reason. The design philosophy expressed is that units with lower supply and higher individual power are preferable to high-supply trainsoak units.
author: elyot
context: Elyot explaining the rationale for giving Bombardier 4 supply rather than 10, in the context of the broader balance discussion.

insight: Plexo Cell is described by masn6811 as the strongest soak unit in the game, stated approvingly as he considers strong soak units desirable when they are interesting. This is offered as a counterpoint to elyot's preference against powerful soak units.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 pushing back on elyot's stated dislike of strong soak units by citing Plexo Cell as his favorite example.

insight: Masn6811 argues that single-X cost is appropriate for blue's color identity in terms of absorb denial, because blue can set up the condition (S hold) to deny one absorb with a single X. Costing multiple X is described as red's slice of the pie, requiring blue to pre-set up if it wants to deny multiple absorb.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion on Lancetooth cost design and whether it should cost X or XX, with masn6811 articulating the color-pie reasoning for single-X blue units.

insight: Ferritin Sac's main use cases are identified as: small red builds, big red builds, and tech-heavy unit lines such as Centurion and Wincer. A BT1 Ferritin Sac is considered potentially broken at 1R->1B conversion but may be acceptable at 3R->3B, since the higher red cost prevents easy chaining and makes it harder to abuse in big red.
author: masn6811
context: Extended debate among masn6811, silentslayers, anesotericman, and elyot on what buff Ferritin Sac needs and how to avoid making big red too strong.

insight: silentslayers reports matching masn6811's viability test results at a 99% rate across almost every unit tested, establishing masn6811 as a highly reliable authority on unit viability assessments within the community.
author: silentslayers
context: silentslayers vouching for the accuracy of masn6811's viability testing methodology during the Ferritin Sac discussion.
